element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: <details>/<summary> for expandable questions, <search> wrapping the form, <code> for the card ID reference, <a href='tel:'> for the phone number, and <section> (not <address>) for the 'Still need help?' block. No div/span used where a semantic element exists.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used only where necessary: aria-label on both <nav> elements (required), aria-labelledby on all <section> elements, aria-current='page' on the breadcrumb current item, and aria-label on <search> to describe its purpose. No redundant roles; tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct for skip-link target.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Frequently Asked Questions'), all three FAQ categories and the 'Still need help?' section correctly use <h2>, no levels are skipped, and no heading tags are used for decorative or visual-only purposes.

landmark_structure.justification: Two skip links ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to FAQ search') are the first focusable elements. <header>, <nav>, <main>, <search>, labelled <section> elements, and <footer> are all used correctly. The site header and footer are outside <main>; the breadcrumb nav inside <main> is appropriate for page-specific navigation.

form_semantics.justification: The search input has a visible <label> with a matching for/id pair, uses type='search', and includes a descriptive placeholder. autocomplete='off' is set appropriately. Simple single-field form; no fieldset needed.

content_realism.justification: All questions and answers are realistic, specific, and varied in length. No placeholder or Lorem ipsum content; pricing figures, plan names, and workflow steps are plausible for a real SaaS product.

list_semantics.justification: Breadcrumb items are correctly in an <ol> (order matters), site navigation items are in a <ul> within <nav>. No lists used where count is irrelevant.

expectations_met: <main> wraps only the FAQ content — site header and footer are outside it
Two skip links ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to FAQ search') are the first focusable elements
Individual questions use <details>/<summary> for progressive disclosure
Questions are NOT marked up as <dl>
Expandable pattern uses native <details>/<summary>, not custom ARIA disclosure
Search/filter input has a visible <label> ('Search questions')
Contact info does not use <address> (correct — it's support contact, not site author info)
additional_observations: Good use of the <search> element with an aria-label to wrap the filter form
All <section> elements carry aria-labelledby, making them proper landmark regions
tabindex='-1' on <main> is the correct pattern to allow programmatic focus from the skip link
